clarified_problem_statement: Rebuild the blind-box item library around 20 scene-plus-purpose entry points and a five-layer content model so the default output is more visible, easier to place, and safer for image-difference prompts.

confirmed_target_users.name: Content Maintainer
needs: rules for assigning items into five pools
pain_points: quality review rules are not explicit

confirmed_target_users.name: Tool User
needs: items that are visible and easy to circle
no change to current input habits
pain_points: some outputs do not fit the target image
small items can be too tiny or unsupported
category choice is slow when names are vague

confirmed_target_users.name: App Maintainer
needs: clear mapping from the new model back to the current runtime schema
pain_points: runtime logic is tightly bound to four bucket keys
there is no blind-box data quality test layer

confirmed_domain: local tkinter desktop tool content taxonomy and static data library

confirmed_features.name: 20 scene-plus-purpose categories
confirmed_features.description: Define 20 short category names such as desk-study, table-tea-break, beach-vacation, and underwater-diving.
acceptance_criteria: A category table lists all 20 categories and scene hints.
The underwater-diving category remains as a special theme entry.
edge_cases: Some legacy themes must be merged instead of renamed one-to-one.
Legacy numeric box ids may need compatibility mapping.

confirmed_features.name: five-layer item pool model
confirmed_features.description: Each category is structured as core_items, support_items, visible_small_items, conditional_items, and blocked_or_risky.
acceptance_criteria: Each layer has scope, entry rules, and exclusion rules.
visible_small_items must be grouped, blocky, or have a clear support surface.
blocked_or_risky items never enter default output.
edge_cases: Some items can shift between visible_small and risky depending on visibility.
The underwater category must exclude animal bodies and lighting effects.

confirmed_features.name: compatibility mapping
confirmed_features.description: Map the five-layer content model back to large, medium, small, and hanging without breaking the current UI.
acceptance_criteria: medium maps to main clear items
hanging maps only to low-weight conditional items
edge_cases: draw_history keys are tied to box id and bucket key
history reset or migration must be decided later if runtime categories change

confirmed_features.name: three pilot category rewrites
confirmed_features.description: Rewrite desk-study, underwater-diving, and park-picnic first.
acceptance_criteria: Each pilot contains all five pools.
Each pilot can be mapped to the current four runtime buckets.
The rest of the library expands only after pilot quality is proven.
edge_cases: underwater-diving must avoid glow, reflections, transparency, bubbles, and animal bodies
outdoor nature items must be grouped or clearly blocky

confirmed_features.name: quality and risk rules
confirmed_features.description: Document entry criteria for safe default items and isolation criteria for risky items.
acceptance_criteria: core items are recognizable at first glance and independently selectable
risky lists include lights, mirrors, transparent reflective objects, fine strings, tassels, edge lines, and animal bodies
category lists do not rely on repeated container suffixes for volume
edge_cases: item_states may push safe items into reflective or transparent language
some conditional items are valid only with clear support surfaces

non_functional_requirements.details: Category names must stay short and easy to scan in a Chinese UI.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_criteria: All 20 names follow a compact scene-plus-purpose pattern.

non_functional_requirements.details: Short-term behavior must preserve current input syntax, runtime buckets, and history semantics.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_criteria: Existing examples still parse without schema errors.

non_functional_requirements.details: The data model must support gradual per-category rollout.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_criteria: Each pilot can be reviewed and replaced independently.

non_functional_requirements.details: Default output must significantly reduce low-visibility and risky items.
non_functional_requirements.measurable_criteria: In 30 pilot samples, obviously unsuitable items stay below 10 percent and blocked_or_risky never appears in default output.

out_of_scope: full rewrite of all 20 production categories right now
changes to animal or expression history semantics
automatic category selection from image understanding
new app release or app version change
constraints: keep the local tkinter tool scope
keep data-only changes in the data layer
preserve current large medium small hanging runtime output in the short term
avoid light, reflection, transparency, micro-traces, fine strings, and animal bodies in default pools

key_assumptions: pilot quality matters more than immediate full replacement
short-term implementation will preserve current UI and input syntax
runtime structure upgrades can be decided after pilots are validated

discussion_log.agent_prompt: Use brainstorm handoff artifacts as confirmed input.
discussion_log.user_response: The spec-generator run was started from the brainstorm handoff path.
